name: cutadapt

metadata.name: cutadapt
metadata.summary: Adapter trimming and other preprocessing of high-throughput sequencing reads

documentation:

```{image} https://github.com/marcelm/cutadapt/workflows/CI/badge.svg
:alt: true
```



```{image} https://img.shields.io/badge/install%20with-bioconda-brightgreen.svg?style=flat
:alt: install with bioconda
:target: http://bioconda.github.io/recipes/cutadapt/README.html
```

# Cutadapt

Cutadapt finds and removes adapter sequences, primers, poly-A tails and other
types of unwanted sequence from your high-throughput sequencing reads.

Cleaning your data in this way is often required: Reads from small-RNA
sequencing contain the 3’ sequencing adapter because the read is longer than
the molecule that is sequenced. Amplicon reads start with a primer sequence.
Poly-A tails are useful for pulling out RNA from your sample, but often you
don’t want them to be in your reads.

Cutadapt helps with these trimming tasks by finding the adapter or primer
sequences in an error-tolerant way. It can also modify and filter single-end
and paired-end reads in various ways. Adapter sequences can contain IUPAC
wildcard characters. Cutadapt can also demultiplex your reads.

Cutadapt is available under the terms of the MIT license.

Cutadapt development was started at [TU Dortmund University](https://www.tu-dortmund.de)
in the group of [Prof. Dr. Sven Rahmann](https://www.rahmannlab.de/).
It is currently being developed within
[NBIS (National Bioinformatics Infrastructure Sweden)](https://nbis.se/).

If you use Cutadapt, please cite
[DOI:10.14806/ej.17.1.200](http://dx.doi.org/10.14806/ej.17.1.200) .

## Links

- [Documentation](https://cutadapt.readthedocs.io/)
- [Source code](https://github.com/marcelm/cutadapt/)
- [Report an issue](https://github.com/marcelm/cutadapt/issues)
- [Project page on PyPI (Python package index)](https://pypi.python.org/pypi/cutadapt/)
- [Wrapper for the Galaxy platform](https://github.com/galaxyproject/tools-iuc/tree/main/tools/cutadapt)

functions.name: cutadapt.utils.available_cpu_count
documentation:
Return the number of available virtual or physical CPUs on this system.
The number of available CPUs can be smaller than the total number of CPUs
when the cpuset(7) mechanism is in use, as is the case on some cluster
systems.

Adapted from http://stackoverflow.com/a/1006301/715090

functions.name: cutadapt.tokenizer.tokenize_braces
documentation:
```pycon
>>> list(tokenize_braces(""))
[]
>>> list(tokenize_braces("before {braced} after"))
[StringToken("before "), BraceToken("braced"), StringToken(" after")]
>>> list(tokenize_braces("ab{cd}{ef}"))
[StringToken("ab"), BraceToken("cd"), BraceToken("ef")]
```

functions.name: cutadapt._match_tables._acgt_table
documentation:
Return a translation table that maps A, C, G, T characters to the lower
four bits of a byte. Other characters (including possibly IUPAC characters)
are mapped to the most significant bit (0x80).

Lowercase versions are also translated, and U is treated the same as T.

functions.name: cutadapt._match_tables._iupac_table
documentation:
Return a translation table for IUPAC characters.

The table maps ASCII-encoded IUPAC nucleotide characters to bytes in which
the four least significant bits are used to represent one nucleotide each.

For the "N" wildcard, additionally the most significant bit is set (0x80),
which allows it to match characters that are not A, C, G or T if _acgt_table
was used to encode them.

Whether two encoded characters x and y match can then be checked with the
expression "x & y != 0".

n -- total no. of reads.

methods.name: cutadapt.report.Statistics.as_json
documentation:
Return a dict representation suitable for dumping in JSON format

To achieve a more compact representation, set one_line to True, which
will wrap some items in a `cutadapt.json.OneLine` object, and use
`cutadapt.json.dumps` instead of `json.dumps` to dump the dict.

classes.name: cutadapt.report.ErrorRanges

methods.name: cutadapt.report.ErrorRanges.__str__
documentation:
```pycon
>>> str(ErrorRanges(length=8, error_rate=0.1))
'1-8 bp: 0'
>>> str(ErrorRanges(length=20, error_rate=0.1))
'1-9 bp: 0; 10-19 bp: 1; 20 bp: 2'
>>> str(ErrorRanges(length=23, error_rate=0.1))
'1-9 bp: 0; 10-19 bp: 1; 20-23 bp: 2'
```

Representation of the lengths up to which a number of errors is allowed
for partial adapter matches.

```pycon
>>> ErrorRanges(length=8, error_rate=0.1).lengths()
[8]
>>> ErrorRanges(length=19, error_rate=0.1).lengths()
[9, 19]
>>> ErrorRanges(length=20, error_rate=0.1).lengths()
[9, 19, 20]
>>> ErrorRanges(length=21, error_rate=0.1).lengths()
[9, 19, 21]
```

The entry at index i in the returned list is the length up to which
i errors are allowed. For example, the list [9, 19, 23] describes that
\- 0 errors are allowed up to length 9
\- 1 error is allowed up to length 19
\- 2 errors are allowed up to length 23

The last number in the list is always the length of the adapter sequence.

classes.name: cutadapt.report.HistogramRow

modules.name: cutadapt.steps
documentation:
Steps of the read output pipeline

After all read modifications have been done, a read is written to at
most one output file. For this, a pipeline represented as a list of "steps"
(SingleEndSteps or PairedEndSteps) is used. Each pipeline step can consume
(discard) a read or pass it on to the next step.

Steps are added to the pipeline in a certain order:

1. First RestFileWriter, InfoFileWriter, WildcardFileWriter because
   they should see all reads before filtering.
2. Filters come next. These are implemented as SingleEndFilter or PairedEndFilter
   instances with an appropriate Predicate. Filters can optionally send each
   consumed/filtered read to an output file.
3. The last pipeline step should be one of the "Sinks", which consume all reads.
   Demultiplexers are sinks, for example.

methods.name: cutadapt.steps.SingleEndStep.__call__
documentation:
Process a single read. Return the processed read or None to indicate that
the read has been consumed and should thus not be passed on to subsequent
steps.

methods.name: cutadapt.steps.PairedEndStep.__call__
documentation:
Process (read1, read2). Return the processed read pair or None if
the read pair has been "consumed" (filtered or written to an output file)
and should thus not be passed on to subsequent steps.

classes.name: cutadapt.steps.HasStatistics
documentation: Used for the final steps (sinks), which need to keep track of read length statistics

methods.name: cutadapt.steps.HasFilterStatistics.filtered
documentation: Return number of filtered reads or read pairs

methods.name: cutadapt.steps.HasFilterStatistics.descriptive_identifier
documentation: Name used in statistics

classes.name: cutadapt.steps.SingleEndFilter
documentation:
A pipeline step that can filter reads, can redirect filtered ones to a writer, and
counts how many were filtered.

classes.name: cutadapt.steps.PairedEndFilter

methods.name: cutadapt.steps.PairedEndFilter.__init__
documentation:
pair_filter_mode -- these values are allowed:
    'any': The pair is discarded if any read matches.
    'both': The pair is discarded if both reads match.
    'first': The pair is discarded if the first read matches.

A pipeline step that can filter paired-end reads, redirect them to a file, and counts
how many read pairs were filtered.

Different filtering styles are supported, differing by which of the
two reads in a pair have to fulfill the filtering criterion.

classes.name: cutadapt.steps.PairedSingleEndStep
documentation:
Wrap a SingleEndStep as a PairedEndStep

The wrapped step is called with the first read

classes.name: cutadapt.steps.SingleEndSink
documentation:
Send each read to a writer and keep read length statistics.
This is used as the last step in a pipeline.
The following steps are used as final step in a pipeline.
They send each read or read pair to the final intended output file,
and they all track the lengths of written reads.

classes.name: cutadapt.steps.PairedEndSink
documentation:
Send each read pair to a writer and keep read length statistics.
This is used as the last step in a pipeline.

classes.name: cutadapt.steps.Demultiplexer

documentation: writers maps an adapter name to a writer

methods.name: cutadapt.steps.Demultiplexer.__call__
documentation: Write the read to the proper output file according to the most recent match

Demultiplex trimmed reads. Reads are written to different output files
depending on which adapter matches.

classes.name: cutadapt.steps.PairedDemultiplexer
documentation:
Demultiplex trimmed paired-end reads. Reads are written to different output files
depending on which adapter (in read 1) matches.

classes.name: cutadapt.steps.CombinatorialDemultiplexer

methods.name: cutadapt.steps.CombinatorialDemultiplexer.__init__
documentation:
Adapter names of the matches on R1 and R2 will be used to look up the writer in the
writers dict. If there is no match on a read, None is used in the lookup instead
of the name. Missing dictionary keys are ignored and can be used to discard
read pairs.

methods.name: cutadapt.steps.CombinatorialDemultiplexer.__call__
documentation:
Write the read to the proper output file according to the most recent matches both on
R1 and R2

Demultiplex paired-end reads depending on which adapter matches, taking into account
matches on R1 and R2.

functions.name: cutadapt.runners.make_runner
documentation:
Run a pipeline.

This uses a SerialPipelineRunner if cores is 1 and a ParallelPipelineRunner otherwise.

Args:
    inpaths:
    cores: number of cores to run the pipeline on (this is actually the number of worker
        processes, there will be one extra process for reading the input file(s))
    buffer_size: Forwarded to `ParallelPipelineRunner()`. Ignored if cores is 1.

variables.name: cutadapt.runners.mpctx_Process
documentation: pragma: no cover

classes.name: cutadapt.runners.ReaderProcess

methods.name: cutadapt.runners.ReaderProcess.__init__
documentation:
Args:
    paths: path to input files
    connections: a list of Connection objects, one for each worker.
    queue: a Queue of worker indices. A worker writes its own index into this
        queue to notify the reader that it is ready to receive more data.
    buffer_size:
    stdin_fd:

Note:
    This expects the paths to the input files as strings because these can be pickled
    while file-like objects such as BufferedReader cannot. When using multiprocessing with
    the "spawn" method, which is the default method on macOS, function arguments must be
    picklable.

Read chunks of FASTA or FASTQ data (single-end or paired) and send them to a worker.

The reader repeatedly

- reads a chunk from the file(s)
- reads a worker index from the Queue
- sends the chunk to connections[index]

and finally sends the stop token -1 ("poison pills") to all connections.

classes.name: cutadapt.runners.WorkerProcess
documentation:
The worker repeatedly reads chunks of data from the read_pipe, runs the pipeline on it
and sends the processed chunks to the write_pipe.

To notify the reader process that it wants data, it puts its own identifier into the
need_work_queue before attempting to read data from the read_pipe.

classes.name: cutadapt.runners.OrderedChunkWriter
documentation:
We may receive chunks of processed data from worker processes
in any order. This class writes them to an output file in
the correct order.

classes.name: cutadapt.runners.PipelineRunner

methods.name: cutadapt.runners.PipelineRunner.run
documentation:
progress: Use an object that supports .update() and .close() such
as DummyProgress, cutadapt.utils.Progress or a tqdm instance
A read processing pipeline

classes.name: cutadapt.runners.ParallelPipelineRunner

methods.name: cutadapt.runners.ParallelPipelineRunner._try_receive
documentation:
Try to receive data over `connection` and return it.
If an exception was received, raise it.

Run a Pipeline in parallel

- At construction, a reader process is spawned.
- When run() is called, as many worker processes as requested are spawned.
- In the main process, results are written to the output files in the correct
  order, and statistics are aggregated.

If a worker needs work, it puts its own index into a Queue() (_need_work_queue).
The reader process listens on this queue and sends the raw data to the
worker that has requested work. For sending the data from reader to worker,
a Connection() is used. There is one such connection for each worker (self._pipes).

For sending the processed data from the worker to the main process, there
is a second set of connections, again one for each worker.

When the reader is finished, it sends 'poison pills' to all workers.
When a worker receives this, it sends a poison pill to the main process,
followed by a Statistics object that contains statistics about all the reads
processed by that worker.

classes.name: cutadapt.runners.SerialPipelineRunner
documentation: Run a Pipeline on a single core

modules.name: cutadapt.predicates
documentation: Filtering criteria (predicates)

methods.name: cutadapt.predicates.Predicate.test
documentation: Return True if the filtering criterion matches.

methods.name: cutadapt.predicates.Predicate.descriptive_identifier
documentation:
Return a short name for this predicate based on the class name such as "too_long",
"too_many_expected_errors".
This is used as identifier in the JSON report.

classes.name: cutadapt.predicates.TooShort
documentation: Select reads that are shorter than the specified minimum length

classes.name: cutadapt.predicates.TooLong
documentation: Select reads that are longer than the specified maximum length

classes.name: cutadapt.predicates.TooManyExpectedErrors
documentation:
Select reads whose expected number of errors, according to the quality
values, exceeds a threshold.

The idea comes from usearch's -fastq_maxee parameter
(http://drive5.com/usearch/).

classes.name: cutadapt.predicates.TooHighAverageErrorRate
documentation:
Select reads that have an average error rate above the threshold.
This works better than TooManyExpectedErrors for reads that are expected to
have varying lengths, such as for long read sequencing technologies.

classes.name: cutadapt.predicates.TooManyN

methods.name: cutadapt.predicates.TooManyN.__init__
documentation:
count -- if it is below 1.0, it will be considered a proportion, and above and equal to
1 will be considered as discarding reads with a number of N's greater than this cutoff.

Select reads that have too many 'N' bases.

Both a raw count or a proportion (relative to the sequence length) can be used.

classes.name: cutadapt.predicates.CasavaFiltered
documentation:
Select reads that have failed the CASAVA filter according to the read header.
The headers look like `xxxx x:Y:x:x` (with a `Y`). Reads that pass the filter
have an `N` instead of `Y`.

Reads with unrecognized headers are not selected.

classes.name: cutadapt.predicates.IsUntrimmed
documentation: Select reads for which no adapter match was found

classes.name: cutadapt.predicates.IsTrimmed
documentation: Select reads for which at least one adapter match was found

functions.name: cutadapt.align.edit_distance
documentation:
Return the edit distance between the strings s and t.
The edit distance is the sum of the numbers of insertions, deletions,
and mismatches that is minimally necessary to transform one string
into the other.

functions.name: cutadapt.align.hamming_environment
documentation:
Find all strings t for which the hamming distance between s and t is at most k,
assuming the alphabet is A, C, G, T.

Yield tuples (t, e, m), where e is the hamming distance between s and t and
m is the number of matches (equal to len(t) - e).

functions.name: cutadapt.align.naive_edit_environment
documentation:
Apply all possible edits up to edit distance k to string s
and yield the resulting strings.
A string may be returned more than once.

functions.name: cutadapt.align.py_edit_environment
documentation:
Find all strings t for which the edit distance between s and t is at most k,
assuming the alphabet is A, C, G, T.

Yield tuples (t, e, score), where e is the edit distance between s and t and
score is the score of the optimal alignment.

functions.name: cutadapt.align.slow_edit_environment
documentation:
Find all strings t for which the edit distance between s and t is at most k,
assuming the alphabet is A, C, G, T.

Yield tuples (t, e, m), where e is the edit distance between s and t and
m is the number of matches in the optimal alignment.

This is slow and only used in testing.

classes.name: cutadapt.align.EndSkip

class_variables.name: cutadapt.align.EndSkip.REFERENCE_START

class_variables.name: cutadapt.align.EndSkip.QUERY_START

class_variables.name: cutadapt.align.EndSkip.REFERENCE_END

modules.name: cutadapt.adapters
documentation:
Adapter finding and trimming classes

The ...Adapter classes are responsible for finding adapters.
The ...Match classes trim the reads.

functions.name: cutadapt.adapters.print_matrices
documentation: For debugging

functions.name: cutadapt.adapters.remainder
documentation:
Determine which section of the read would not be trimmed. Return a tuple (start, stop)
that gives the interval of the untrimmed part relative to the original read.

matches must be non-empty

classes.name: cutadapt.adapters.Where
documentation:
Aligner flag combinations for all adapter types.

"REFERENCE" is the adapter sequence, "QUERY" is the read sequence
TODO remove this enum, this should be within each Adapter class

classes.name: cutadapt.adapters.EndStatistics

methods.name: cutadapt.adapters.EndStatistics.random_match_probabilities
documentation:
Estimate probabilities that this adapter end matches a
random sequence. Indels are not taken into account.

Returns a list p, where p[i] is the probability that
i bases of this adapter match a random sequence with
GC content gc_content.
Statistics about the 5' or 3' end

classes.name: cutadapt.adapters.SingleAdapterStatistics
documentation:
Statistics about a 5' or 3' adapter, where we only need to keep track of sequences
removed from one "end".

classes.name: cutadapt.adapters.LinkedAdapterStatistics
documentation: Statistics about sequences removed by a lined adapter.

classes.name: cutadapt.adapters.AnywhereAdapterStatistics
documentation: Statistics about sequences removed by a lined adapter.

classes.name: cutadapt.adapters.SingleMatch

methods.name: cutadapt.adapters.SingleMatch.wildcards
documentation:
Return a string that contains, for each wildcard character,
the character that it matches. For example, if the adapter
ATNGNA matches ATCGTA, then the string 'CT' is returned.

If there are indels, this is not reliable as the full alignment
is not available.
Representation of a single adapter matched to a single string

classes.name: cutadapt.adapters.RemoveBeforeMatch

methods.name: cutadapt.adapters.RemoveBeforeMatch.rest
documentation:
Return the part of the read before this match if this is a
'front' (5') adapter,
return the part after the match if this is not a 'front' adapter (3').
This can be an empty string.

methods.name: cutadapt.adapters.RemoveBeforeMatch.remainder_interval
documentation:
Return an interval (start, stop) that describes the part of the read that would
remain after trimming
A match that removes sequence before the match

classes.name: cutadapt.adapters.RemoveAfterMatch

methods.name: cutadapt.adapters.RemoveAfterMatch.rest
documentation:
Return the part of the read before this match if this is a
'front' (5') adapter,
return the part after the match if this is not a 'front' adapter (3').
This can be an empty string.

methods.name: cutadapt.adapters.RemoveAfterMatch.remainder_interval
documentation:
Return an interval (start, stop) that describes the part of the read that would
remain after trimming
A match that removes sequence after the match

classes.name: cutadapt.adapters.Matchable
documentation: Something that has a match_to() method.

methods.name: cutadapt.adapters.Adapter.spec
documentation: Return string representation of this adapter

class_variables.name: cutadapt.adapters.Adapter.description
documentation: this is overriden in subclasses

classes.name: cutadapt.adapters.SingleAdapter

methods.name: cutadapt.adapters.SingleAdapter.enable_debug
documentation:
Print out the dynamic programming matrix after matching a read to an
adapter.

methods.name: cutadapt.adapters.SingleAdapter.match_to
documentation:
Attempt to match this adapter to the given string.

Return a Match instance if a match was found;
return None if no match was found given the matching criteria (minimum
overlap length, maximum error rate).

This class is used to find a single adapter characterized by sequence, error rate,
type etc. within reads.

Arguments:
    sequence (str): The adapter sequence. Will be converted to uppercase.
        Also, Us will be converted to Ts.

    max_errors: Maximum allowed errors (non-negative float). If the values is less than 1, this
        is interpreted as a rate and passed to the aligner. If it is 1 or greater, the value
        is converted to a rate by dividing it by the number of non-N characters in the sequence.

    The error rate is the number of errors in the alignment divided by the length
    of the part of the alignment that matches the adapter.

    min_overlap: Report a match only if at least this number of bases of the adapter are
        aligned to the read.

    read_wildcards: Whether IUPAC wildcards in the read are allowed.

    adapter_wildcards: Whether IUPAC wildcards in the adapter are allowed.

    name: Optional name of the adapter. If not provided, the name is set to a
        unique number.

    indels: Whether indels are allowed in the alignment.

classes.name: cutadapt.adapters.FrontAdapter

methods.name: cutadapt.adapters.FrontAdapter.match_to
documentation:
Attempt to match this adapter to the given read.

Return a Match instance if a match was found;

documentation: def __init__(self, *args, **kwargs):
self._force_anywhere = kwargs.pop("force_anywhere", False)
super().__init__(*args, **kwargs)

documentation:
Attempt to match this adapter to the given read.

Return a Match instance if a match was found;

Attempt to match this adapter to the given read.

Return a Match instance if a match was found;
return None if no match was found given the matching criteria (minimum
overlap length, maximum error rate).

classes.name: cutadapt.adapters.AnywhereAdapter

methods.name: cutadapt.adapters.AnywhereAdapter.match_to
documentation:
Attempt to match this adapter to the given string.

Return a Match instance if a match was found;
return None if no match was found given the matching criteria (minimum
overlap length, maximum error rate).

An adapter that can be 5' or 3'. If a match involves the first base of
the read, it is assumed to be a 5' adapter and a 3' otherwise.

classes.name: cutadapt.adapters.AdapterIndex

methods.name: cutadapt.adapters.AdapterIndex.__init__
documentation: All given adapters must be of the same type

methods.name: cutadapt.adapters.AdapterIndex._accept
documentation: Raise a ValueError if the adapter is not acceptable

methods.name: cutadapt.adapters.AdapterIndex.is_acceptable
documentation:
Return whether this adapter is acceptable for being used in an index

Adapters are not acceptable if they allow wildcards, allow too many errors,
or would lead to a very large index.

methods.name: cutadapt.adapters.AdapterIndex._match_to_one_length
documentation:
Match a query string against all adapters and return a Match that represents
the best match or None if no match was found

methods.name: cutadapt.adapters.AdapterIndex._match_to_multiple_lengths
documentation:
Match the adapters against a string and return a Match that represents
the best match or None if no match was found

Index of multiple adapters

Represent multiple adapters of the same type at once and use an index data structure
to speed up matching. This is faster than iterating over multiple adapters.

There are quite a few restrictions:
- the error rate allows at most 2 mismatches
- wildcards in the adapter are not allowed
- wildcards in the read are not allowed

Use the is_acceptable() method to check individual adapters.

modules.name: cutadapt.modifiers
documentation:
This module implements all the read modifications that cutadapt supports.
A modifier must be callable and typically implemented as a class with a
__call__ method.

classes.name: cutadapt.modifiers.PairedEndModifierWrapper

methods.name: cutadapt.modifiers.PairedEndModifierWrapper.__init__
documentation: Set one of the modifiers to None to work on R1 or R2 only
Wrap two SingleEndModifiers that work on both reads in a paired-end read

classes.name: cutadapt.modifiers.AdapterCutter

methods.name: cutadapt.modifiers.AdapterCutter._split_adapters
documentation:
Split adapters into three different categories so that they can possibly be used
with a MultiAdapter. Return a tuple (prefix, suffix, other), where
- prefix is a list of all anchored 5' adapters that MultiAdapter would accept
- suffix is a list of all anchored 3' adapters that MultiAdapter would accept
- other is a list of all remaining adapters.

methods.name: cutadapt.modifiers.AdapterCutter.match_and_trim
documentation:
Search for the best-matching adapter in a read, perform the requested action
('trim', 'mask' etc. as determined by self.action) and return the
(possibly) modified read.

*self.times* adapter removal rounds are done. During each round,
only the best-matching adapter is trimmed. If no adapter was found in a round,
no further rounds are attempted.

Return a pair (trimmed_read, matches), where matches is a list of Match instances.

methods.name: cutadapt.modifiers.AdapterCutter._match_and_trim_once_action_trim
documentation: Specalization of match_and_trim for the case that self.times == 1 and self.action == 'trim'

Repeatedly find one of multiple adapters in reads.

Arguments:
    adapters: Adapters to be searched
    times: Repeat the search this number of times.
    action: What to do with a found adapter.
        - *None*: Do nothing, only update the ModificationInfo appropriately
        - "trim": Remove the adapter and down- or upstream sequence depending on adapter type
        - "mask": Replace the part of the sequence that would have been removed with "N" bases
        - "lowercase": Convert the part of the sequence that would have been removed to lowercase
        - "retain": Like "trim", but leave the adapter sequence itself in the read
    index: If True, attempt to create an index to speed up the search (if possible)

classes.name: cutadapt.modifiers.ReverseComplementer

methods.name: cutadapt.modifiers.ReverseComplementer.__init__
documentation: rc_suffix -- suffix to add to the read name if sequence was reverse-complemented
Trim adapters from a read and its reverse complement

classes.name: cutadapt.modifiers.PairedReverseComplementer

methods.name: cutadapt.modifiers.PairedReverseComplementer.__init__
documentation: rc_suffix -- suffix to add to the read name if sequence was reverse-complemented
Trim adapters from a read pair or its reverse complement (R1/R2 swapped)

classes.name: cutadapt.modifiers.PairedAdapterCutter

methods.name: cutadapt.modifiers.PairedAdapterCutter.__init__
documentation:
adapters1 -- list of Adapters to be removed from R1
adapters2 -- list of Adapters to be removed from R2

Both lists must have the same, non-zero length.
 read pair is trimmed if adapters1[i] is found in R1 and adapters2[i] in R2.

action -- What to do with a found adapter: None, 'trim', 'lowercase' or 'mask'
Trim adapters in pairs from R1 and R2.

classes.name: cutadapt.modifiers.UnconditionalCutter
documentation:
A modifier that unconditionally removes the first n or the last n bases from a read.

If the length is positive, the bases are removed from the beginning of the read.
If the length is negative, the bases are removed from the end of the read.

classes.name: cutadapt.modifiers.LengthTagModifier
documentation: Replace "length=..." strings in read names.

classes.name: cutadapt.modifiers.SuffixRemover
documentation: Remove a given suffix from read names.

classes.name: cutadapt.modifiers.PrefixSuffixAdder
documentation: Add a suffix and a prefix to read names

classes.name: cutadapt.modifiers.Renamer

methods.name: cutadapt.modifiers.Renamer.compile_rename_function
documentation:
Create the function that computes a new name

By creating the code dynamically, we can ensure that only those placeholder values are
computed that are actually used in the template.

methods.name: cutadapt.modifiers.Renamer.parse_name
documentation: Parse read header and return (id, comment) tuple

Rename reads using a template

The template string can contain the following placeholders:

- {header} -- full, unchanged header
- {id} -- the part of the header before the first whitespace
- {comment} -- the part of the header after the ID, excluding initial whitespace
- {cut_prefix} -- prefix removed by UnconditionalCutter (with positive length argument)
- {cut_suffix} -- suffix removed by UnconditionalCutter (with negative length argument)
- {adapter_name} -- name of the *last* adapter match or no_adapter if there was none
- {match_sequence} -- the sequence that matched the adapter (this includes possible errors)
      or an empty string if there was no match
- {rc} -- the string 'rc' if the read was reverse complemented (with --revcomp) or '' otherwise

classes.name: cutadapt.modifiers.PairedEndRenamer
documentation:
Rename paired-end reads using a template. The template is applied to both
R1 and R2, and the same template variables as in the (single-end) renamer
are allowed. However,
these variables are evaluated separately for each read. For example, if `{comment}`
is used, it gets replaced with the R1 comment in the R1 header, and with the R2
comment in the R2 header.

Additionally, all template variables except `id` can be used in the read-specific
forms `{r1.variablename}` and `{r2.variablename}`. For example, `{r1.comment}`
always gets replaced with the R1 comment, even in R2.

classes.name: cutadapt.modifiers.ZeroCapper
documentation: Change negative quality values of a read to zero

classes.name: cutadapt.modifiers.PolyATrimmer
documentation: Trim poly-A tails or poly-T heads

classes.name: cutadapt.modifiers.Shortener
documentation:
Unconditionally shorten a read to the given length

If the length is positive, the bases are removed from the end of the read.
If the length is negative, the bases are removed from the beginning of the read.

classes.name: cutadapt.modifiers.NEndTrimmer
documentation: Trims Ns from the 3' and 5' end of reads

functions.name: cutadapt.kmer_heuristic.kmer_chunks
documentation:
Partition a sequence in almost equal sized chunks. Returns the shortest
possibility. AABCABCABC, 3 returns {"AABC", "ABC"}

functions.name: cutadapt.kmer_heuristic.remove_redundant_kmers
documentation:
This removes kmers that are searched in multiple search sets and makes
sure they are only searched in the larger search set. This reduces the
amount of searched patterns and therefore the number of false positives.

functions.name: cutadapt.kmer_heuristic.create_positions_and_kmers
documentation:
Create a set of position and words combinations where at least one of the
words needs to occur at its specified position. If not an alignment
algorithm will not be able to find a solution. This can be checked very
quickly and allows for skipping alignment in cases where the adapter would
not align anyway.

Example: looking for AAAAATTTTT with at most one error. This means either
AAAAA or TTTTT (or both) must be present, otherwise alignment will not
succeed.

This function returns the positions and the accompanying words while also
taking into account partial overlap for back and front adapters.

functions.name: cutadapt.kmer_heuristic.kmer_probability_analysis
documentation:
Returns a tab separated table with for each kmer a start, stop, the number
of considered sites and the hit chance on a randomly generated sequence
containing only A, C, G and T. Assumes kmers only consist of A, C, G and T
too.

Useful for investigating whether the create_positions_and_kmers function
creates a useful runtime heuristic.

modules.name: cutadapt.cli
documentation:
cutadapt version {version}

Copyright (C) 2010 Marcel Martin <marcel.martin@scilifelab.se> and contributors

Cutadapt removes adapter sequences from high-throughput sequencing reads.

Usage:
    cutadapt -a ADAPTER [options] [-o output.fastq] input.fastq

For paired-end reads:
    cutadapt -a ADAPT1 -A ADAPT2 [options] -o out1.fastq -p out2.fastq in1.fastq in2.fastq

Replace "ADAPTER" with the actual sequence of your 3' adapter. IUPAC wildcard
characters are supported. All reads from input.fastq will be written to
output.fastq with the adapter sequence removed. Adapter matching is
error-tolerant. Multiple adapter sequences can be given (use further -a
options), but only the best-matching adapter will be removed.

Input may also be in FASTA format. Compressed input and output is supported and
auto-detected from the file name (.gz, .xz, .bz2). Use the file name '-' for
standard input/output. Without the -o option, output is sent to standard output.

Citation:

Marcel Martin. Cutadapt removes adapter sequences from high-throughput
sequencing reads. EMBnet.Journal, 17(1):10-12, May 2011.
http://dx.doi.org/10.14806/ej.17.1.200

Run "cutadapt --help" to see all command-line options.
See https://cutadapt.readthedocs.io/ for full documentation.
!/usr/bin/env python
Copyright (c) 2010 Marcel Martin <marcel.martin@scilifelab.se> and contributors
Permission is hereby granted, free of charge, to any person obtaining a copy
of this software and associated documentation files (the "Software"), to deal
in the Software without restriction, including without limitation the rights
to use, copy, modify, merge, publish, distribute, sublicense, and/or sell
copies of the Software, and to permit persons to whom the Software is
furnished to do so, subject to the following conditions:
The above copyright notice and this permission notice shall be included in
all copies or substantial portions of the Software.
THE SOFTWARE IS PROVIDED "AS IS", WITHOUT WARRANTY OF ANY KIND, EXPRESS OR
IMPLIED, INCLUDING BUT NOT LIMITED TO THE WARRANTIES OF MERCHANTABILITY,
FITNESS FOR A PARTICULAR PURPOSE AND NONINFRINGEMENT. IN NO EVENT SHALL THE
AUTHORS OR COPYRIGHT HOLDERS BE LIABLE FOR ANY CLAIM, DAMAGES OR OTHER
LIABILITY, WHETHER IN AN ACTION OF CONTRACT, TORT OR OTHERWISE, ARISING FROM,
OUT OF OR IN CONNECTION WITH THE SOFTWARE OR THE USE OR OTHER DEALINGS IN
THE SOFTWARE.

functions.name: cutadapt.cli.get_argument_parser
documentation: fmt: off

functions.name: cutadapt.cli.parse_cutoffs
documentation:
Parse a string INT[,INT] into a pair of integers

```pycon
>>> parse_cutoffs("5")
(0, 5)
>>> parse_cutoffs("6,7")
(6, 7)
```
fmt: on

functions.name: cutadapt.cli.parse_lengths
documentation:
Parse [INT]\[:[INT]\] into a pair of integers. If a value is omitted, use None

```pycon
>>> parse_lengths('25')
(25,)
>>> parse_lengths('17:25')
(17, 25)
>>> parse_lengths('25:')
(25, None)
>>> parse_lengths(':25')
(None, 25)
```

functions.name: cutadapt.cli.determine_demultiplex_mode
documentation: Return one of "normal", "combinatorial" or False

functions.name: cutadapt.cli.determine_paired
documentation: Determine whether we should work in paired-end mode.

functions.name: cutadapt.cli.make_input_paths
documentation: Do some other error checking of the input file names and return InputPaths.

functions.name: cutadapt.cli.make_pipeline_from_args
documentation:
Set up a processing pipeline from parsed command-line arguments.

If there are any problems parsing the arguments, raise a CommandLineError.

Return an instance of Pipeline (SingleEndPipeline or PairedEndPipeline)

functions.name: cutadapt.cli.log_header
documentation: Print the "This is cutadapt ..." header

functions.name: cutadapt.cli.main_cli
documentation: Entry point for command-line script

functions.name: cutadapt.cli.main
documentation:
Set up a processing pipeline from the command-line arguments, run it and return
a Statistics object.

classes.name: cutadapt.cli.CutadaptArgumentParser

methods.name: cutadapt.cli.CutadaptArgumentParser.error
documentation:
If you override this in a subclass, it should not return -- it
should either exit or raise an exception.

This ArgumentParser customizes two things:
- The usage message is not prefixed with 'usage:'
- A brief message is shown on errors, not full usage

functions.name: cutadapt.files.xopen_rb_raise_limit
documentation:
Open a (possibly compressed) file for reading in binary mode, trying to avoid the
"Too many open files" problem using `open_raise_limit`.

functions.name: cutadapt.files.open_raise_limit
documentation:
Run 'func' (which should be some kind of open() function) and return its result.
If "Too many open files" occurs, increase limit and try again.

functions.name: cutadapt.files.detect_file_format
documentation: TODO copied and adjusted from dnaio; upstream this

variables.name: cutadapt.files.resource
documentation: type: ignore

methods.name: cutadapt.files.FileOpener.__init__
documentation:
threads -- no. of external compression threads.
    0: write in-process
    None: min(cpu_count(), 4)

classes.name: cutadapt.files.ProxyTextFile

methods.name: cutadapt.files.ProxyTextFile.__getstate__
documentation: TextIOWrapper cannot be pickled. Just don’t include our state.
A file object for writing in text mode that is backed by a BytesIO object

classes.name: cutadapt.files.ProxyRecordWriter

methods.name: cutadapt.files.ProxyRecordWriter.__getstate__
documentation: Exclude the dnaio Reader class from the state
A writer for FASTA, FASTQ records etc. that is backed by a BytesIO object

methods.name: cutadapt.files.OutputFiles.close
documentation: Close all output files that are not stdout

modules.name: cutadapt.parser
documentation: Parse adapter specifications

functions.name: cutadapt.parser.parse_search_parameters
documentation: Parse key=value;key=value;key=value into a dict

functions.name: cutadapt.parser.expand_braces
documentation:
Replace all occurrences of `x{n}` (where x is any character) with n
occurrences of x. Raise ValueError if the expression cannot be parsed.

```pycon
>>> expand_braces('TGA{5}CT')
'TGAAAAACT'
```

functions.name: cutadapt.parser.make_adapters_from_specifications
documentation:
Create a list of Adapter classes from specification strings and adapter types.

type_spec_pairs -- a list of (str, str) pairs, where the first is
  the adapter type (either 'front', 'back' or 'anywhere') and the second is the
  adapter specification string, such as "ACGT;o=3" or "file:adapters.fasta"

search_parameters -- A dict with default search parameters. These can be overriden by the
  adapter specifications. They are passed as **kwargs when instantiating the
  adapter classes.
  Possible keys: max_error_rate, min_overlap, read_wildcards, adapter_wildcards, indels

Return a list of appropriate Adapter instances.

functions.name: cutadapt.parser.make_adapters_from_one_specification
documentation: Parse an adapter specification and yield appropriate Adapter classes.

functions.name: cutadapt.parser.make_adapter
documentation:
Parse an adapter specification not using `file:` notation and return
an object of an appropriate Adapter class.

name -- Adapter name if not included as part of the spec. (If spec is
'name=ADAPTER', name will be 'name'.)

adapter_type -- describes which commandline parameter was used (`-a`
is 'back', `-b` is 'anywhere', and `-g` is 'front').

search_parameters -- dict with default search parameters

functions.name: cutadapt.parser._make_linked_adapter
documentation: Return a linked adapter from two specification strings

functions.name: cutadapt.parser.read_adapters_fasta
documentation: Read adapter sequences from a FASTA file

classes.name: cutadapt.parser.AdapterSpecification

methods.name: cutadapt.parser.AdapterSpecification._extract_name
documentation: Parse an adapter specification given as 'name=adapt' into 'name' and 'adapt'.

methods.name: cutadapt.parser.AdapterSpecification.parse
documentation:
Parse an adapter specification for a non-linked adapter (without '...')
and return an AdapterSpecification instance.

Allow:
'back' and ADAPTER
'back' and ADAPTERX
'back' and ADAPTER$
'front' and ADAPTER
'front' and XADAPTER
'front' and ^ADAPTER
'anywhere' and ADAPTER

methods.name: cutadapt.parser.AdapterSpecification._restriction_to_class
documentation: restriction: None, "anchored", or "noninternal"

\# noqa: E501
Description of a single non-linked adapter.

These are the attributes:

- name (None or str)
- restriction (None, 'anchored', or 'noninternal')
- sequence (nucleotide sequence as string)
- search parameters (dict with keys such as 'max_errors', 'min_overlap')
- adapter_type ('front' for -a, 'back' for -g and 'anywhere' for -b)

```pycon
>>> AdapterSpecification.parse('a_name=ACGT;anywhere', 'back')
AdapterSpecification(name='a_name', restriction=None, sequence='ACGT', parameters={'anywhere': True}, adapter_type='back')
```
